Metals Comparative Signal
← Back to dashboard

Metals Comparative Signal

Regime-aware, multi-factor cross-metal signal with rolling relative value, z-scores, momentum, and macro USD adjustment.

As of 2026-04-17

Gemini Summary

Signal Summary:

  • Configuration statement: Given the Aluminium z-score of 3.431, Silver relative overvaluation, and a Composite Score of -0.40875, this setup aligns with Downward-biased price paths and Elevated volatility, where the dominant risk is Mean reversion, not Trend continuation.
  • The signal is currently in a "Moderate Outflow Bias" state within a "high_vol_dislocation" regime (1).
  • Conviction Band: High; Interpretation Confidence: High Confidence; Internal Conflict Flag: No. Signal Stability Assessment: Volatile; Threshold Proximity: Near; Revision Sensitivity: Unknown.

Methodology Applied:

  • Z-score Standardisation: Triggers "Tactical Trim" when scores exceed 2.0 and "Exhaustion Risk" above 3.0 (1).
  • Relative Value (RV) Layer: Percentiles above 95.0 indicate an "Overvalued" status for cross-metal ratios (1).
  • Regime Weighting: Under the "high_vol_dislocation" profile, RV is weighted at 40%, while Standardisation and Momentum carry 30% each (1).
  • Metals Comparative Signal data as of 2026-04-17 (1).

Key Dynamics:

  • The primary driver is the extreme Standardisation Layer (-0.5) and RV Layer (-0.8), reflecting broad metals exhaustion (1).
  • Momentum has reached an inflection point with a negative 3-month score of -0.119, despite recent price peaks (1).
  • Internal tensions are low as almost all major metals (Gold, Silver, Copper, Platinum) concurrently signal "Tactical Trim" (1).
  • Conditional Invalidation: A shift in the detected regime out of "high_vol_dislocation" or a sharp reversal in the Silver-to-Gold ratio percentile.
  • The signal is currently volatile, reflecting a rapid move into overextension over the last 15 trading sessions (1).

Scenario Balance:

  • Base Case dominant: Mean reversion and profit-taking driven by exhaustion signals in Aluminium and Copper.
  • Upside risk: A transition to a "low_vol_uptrend" regime if price stability returns at these levels.
  • Downside risk: A "regime shift" toward heavy liquidation if USD strength transitions from "neutral" to "strong".

Time Horizon & Aggregation:

  • Time Horizon: Tactical (weeks) based on the prevalence of "Tactical Trim" diagnostic labels (1).
  • Aggregation Weight Hint: High, as multiple sub-layers (RV and Standardisation) are in deep alignment for a reversal (1).

Macro Relevance:

  • This signal informs the "pricing and sentiment" macro dimension, specifically reflecting potential positioning saturation in industrials and havens.
  • The implied economic mechanism is "positioning pressure," where extreme price-to-volatility dislocations trigger systematic selling.
  • Cycle position: not determined.
  • Typically interacts with USD liquidity signals; a neutral USD factor currently prevents more aggressive outflow scores (1).

Regime Context:

  • The regime is currently persistent in "high_vol_dislocation" without hysteresis activation (1).
  • Direction of change is strengthening toward an "Outflow Bias" as z-scores continue to climb into exhaustion territory (1).

Model Limitations:

  • Revision window cannot be assessed from provided content.
  • Relies on historical ratio percentiles which may shift during structural macro regime changes.

Data & References:

  • Metals Comparative Signal: Latest observation 2026-04-17 (1).
  • Most influential datapoints: Aluminium Z-score (3.431) and Silver/Gold ratio percentile (97.86) (1).
  • Public datasets: LME Warehouse inventory levels and CFTC Commitment of Traders (CoT) reports.

Metals Comparative Dashboard v1.2 (Regime-Adaptive)

Rolling relative value, regime-aware z-scores, momentum and composite layers, plus silver regime score and z-score percentile context.

Methodology
The information on this website is provided for general informational and educational purposes only and does not constitute financial, investment, legal, or tax advice. It does not take into account any individual objectives, financial situation, or needs.

All views expressed are personal, based on publicly available information, and do not represent the views of any employer or reflect any proprietary or internal analysis. This information should not be relied upon for making investment decisions.

No representation or warranty is made as to the accuracy, completeness, or timeliness of the information, and no liability is accepted for any loss arising directly or indirectly from its use.
